text: Abiotic stress happens when living things are affected by non-living factors in their environment. This can include extreme temperatures, droughts, salty soil, or too much or too little light. Too much or too little light also affects how well plants make food. Abiotic stress is a big problem because it hurts crop yields, kills plants, and disrupts entire ecosystems. Scientists are trying to understand how abiotic stress works so they can find ways to help plants be more resilient in tough conditions.

text: Many living things produce tiny particles called spores as they grow and change. This is called sporogenesis. Some creatures, like animals and certain small plants, don't make spores this way. These spores can grow into new plants without needing to be fertilized first. This process is called alternation of generations and it's an important step in creating new plants. It might seem strange because the spore-making part doesn't involve mixing sex cells, but it's still a crucial part of how plants reproduce.

text: Amdahl's law is a way to figure out how much faster a computer program can run if it uses many processors at the same time. Gene Amdahl came up with this idea in 1967 and found that there's a limit to how fast a program can go, even if most of it can be split into smaller tasks for multiple processors. This is because some parts of the program have to run one after the other, which slows everything down. The law says that no matter how many processors you add, there will always be a part of the program that can't be sped up much more. It uses numbers and formulas to show this idea and helps programmers understand why their programs might not get as fast as they want them to, even with lots of powerful computers working together.

text: Confucius was a Chinese philosopher who lived from 551 to 479 BCE. He was born in the state of Lu, which is now part of modern-day Shandong Province. Confucius taught people how to be good citizens and have fair relationships with others. He believed that education and self-improvement are key to personal growth. His ideas had a big impact on East Asian thought and culture, and he's often credited with creating the philosophy of Confucianism. Some scholars think he was more like a teacher who shared existing knowledge rather than an original thinker. His teachings were written down by his students in a book called the Analects, which is still widely read today. Confucius' ideas have influenced many aspects of society, including politics, education, and social norms not just in China but also in other parts of Asia.
label: llm

text: The study of plants is called botany. Botanists look at how plants grow, develop, and make babies, as well as what's happening inside them like how they make food from sunlight and take in nutrients. They also examine the different types of plants, where they live, and how they interact with other living things around them. This study involves many areas of expertise, including using special tools to look at plant cells and molecules up close. Botany has many practical uses, such as helping farmers grow crops better, developing new medicines from plants, and protecting endangered species. By understanding how plants work and live in their environment, botanists can help create new technologies and products made from plants, making it a very important area of study today.

text: Arnulf fought wars against Great Moravia from 892 to 899 but wasn't able to fully conquer it. Despite this, he had some successes during that time. In 895, a part of Great Moravia called Bohemia broke away and became a smaller state under Arnulf's control. This meant that Bohemia was no longer at risk of being attacked by the Franks. Around the same time, Great Moravia likely lost some land to Arnulf, including parts of present-day western Hungary. As a reward for his efforts, someone named Wiching became one of Arnulf's advisors in 892. In 899, Arnulf even asked for help from another group called the Magyars who lived nearby, and with their assistance he was able to gain more control over Moravia.

text: Babrak Karmal was an important Afghan politician from 1979 to 1986. He was born in 1929 and studied law at Kabul University before joining a group called the People's Democratic Party of Afghanistan (PDPA) in the early 1960s. Karmal quickly became a leader within this party and helped stage a revolution that overthrew the monarchy in 1978. After the Soviet Union invaded Afghanistan in 1979, Karmal was chosen as President by the government backed by the Soviets. He tried to make Afghanistan more socialist and modernize its economy, but many people opposed his ideas because they wanted to keep their traditional way of life. The Soviet Union supported Karmal's government with money and military aid, but he eventually resigned in 1986 due to disagreements with the Soviets over how to fight against a group called the mujahideen.

text: Blizzard Entertainment is an American company that makes and publishes video games based in California. It was founded on February 8, 1991, by three people: Mike Morhaime, Frank Pearce, and Allen Adham. At first, their games didn't get much attention, but things changed with "Warcraft: Orcs & Humans" in 1994, a game that made people take notice. They then released more popular titles like "StarCraft" in 1998 and "Diablo II" in 2000, which helped make Blizzard a big player in the gaming industry. One of their most famous games is probably "World of Warcraft," an online multiplayer game released in 2004 that has become one of the best-selling video games ever made. Since then, they have continued to release new and popular games like "Overwatch" and "Hearthstone", which has solidified their position as a leader in the gaming industry.

text: Governments with corruption problems often give special treatment to business owners who know them well. This can also happen because of someone's religion or where they come from. For example, in Syria, the Alawite minority has more influence over the government and businesses there. The leader, President Assad, is an Alawite too. Think of people connected like a big social network. When leaders need help to get what they want, they often turn to others who have power and connections. These powerful individuals become important figures in the network. In many poor countries, these key players are limited, so economic and political power is held by a small group of connected people.
